---
title: "Module 06: Metrics, Imbalance, and SHAP"
---

## Learning objectives

- Choose **scores** that match real costs (false alarm vs missed case), disease **prevalence**, and whether you care about **ranking** people or about **well-calibrated probabilities**.
- Diagnose **class imbalance** without fetishizing accuracy; use sensitivity, specificity, ROC vs PR as appropriate.
- Introduce **SHAP** (SHapley Additive exPlanations) as a way to **attribute** a single prediction to predictors — with **causal** and **correlation** caveats: a big bar does not mean “knocking out this gene cures the disease.”

## Key messages

- **Interpretability complements validation**; neither replaces honest held-out or nested evaluation.
- **Palmer Penguins** supports “what if we look within **species** or **island**?” as a **toy** fairness-style check — with only a few hundred birds, treat it as **classroom pedagogy**, not a regulatory audit.

## Concept outline

### Metrics toolbox

- Accuracy only when costs symmetric and prevalence balanced.
- Precision / recall / F1 when costs asymmetric or positives rare.
- ROC-AUC vs PR-AUC: **ROC** emphasizes ranking across thresholds; **PR** often helps when the **positive class is rare**.
- **Calibration**: do predicted probabilities match observed frequencies?

### Imbalance and thresholds

- Class weights, resampling, threshold tuning; tie to **decision problem** not leaderboard.

### Variable importance and SHAP

- Model-specific importance vs **permutation** importance (shuffle one column, see how much error jumps).
- SHAP values as a Shapley-style **share of the prediction**; **TreeSHAP** vs **KernelSHAP** are two algorithm choices for computing SHAP (speed vs flexibility) — details stay on Thursday slides.
- **Guardrail:** association \(\neq\) intervention; SHAP is not a license to change biology or policy without design.

## Course links

- **Slides:** [Thursday deck](../slides/day-03-thursday.qmd) (metrics, imbalance, SHAP sections)
- **Notebooks — penguins:** [three-species](../notebooks/penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd) (which species is hardest after a train/test split?), [sex](../notebooks/penguins-sex-classification.Rmd) (binary sex after dropping incomplete rows)
- **Data:** [Palmer Penguins](../dataset-penguins.qmd)
- **Curriculum:** Thursday consolidation and humility prompts in [curriculum outline](../curriculum-outline.qmd)
- **Module library:** [Back to all modules](index.qmd)

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
## Draft / TODO

- Add **one** example confusion matrix graphic (static) comparing “overall accuracy” vs **per-class recall** on penguins — or link to Thursday slide anchor.
- Instructor-only link list for **fairlearn**, **iml**, or **shap** Python bridge if the course adds bilingual materials.
:::
